title: Nada Sahib
tagline: Nada Sahib — heritage city of Haryana

overview.short: Nada Sahib is a Sikh gurudwara in the Panchkula district of the Indian state of Haryana.
overview.description: Nada Sahib is a Sikh gurudwara in the Panchkula district of the Indian state of Haryana. Situated on the banks of the Ghaggar-Hakra River in the Sivalik Hills of Panchkula, it is the site where Guru Gobind Singh Ji halted while travelling from Paonta Sahib to Anandpur Sahib after the Battle of Bhangani in 1688.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Tau Devi Lal Athletic Stadium (Panchkula)
topPlaces.description: Tau Devi Lal Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium located in Sector 3, Panchkula, a satellite town in the outskirts of Chandigarh. It is the home ground of the Indian Super League side RoundGlass Punjab FC, together with Guru Nanak Stadium.

topPlaces.name: Tau Devi Lal Cricket Stadium
topPlaces.description: Tau Devi Lal Stadium is a cricket stadium located in Sector 3, Panchkula, a satellite town in the outskirts of Chandigarh. The stadium is capable of holding around 7,000 spectators and came into picture because of the Indian Cricket League's inaugural Twenty20 tournament.

topPlaces.name: Panchkula
topPlaces.description: Panchkula (ISO: Pañckulā) is a city and district headquarter in the Panchkula district in Haryana, India. It is a satellite city of the state capital Chandigarh.

topPlaces.name: National Cactus and Succulent Botanical Garden and Research Centre
topPlaces.description: The National Cactus and Succulent Botanical Garden and Research Centre, known as the Cactus Garden, is a 7 acres (2.8 ha) cactus garden in Sector 5 of Panchkula, Haryana, India, was established in 1987, and is known for its rare and endangered species of Indian succulent plants.

image.alt: National Cactus and Succulent Botanical Garden and Research Centre

topPlaces.name: Haryana State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation
topPlaces.description: Haryana State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation (HSIIDC) (established 8 March 1967), formerly Haryana State Industrial Development Corporation (HSIDC), headquartered at Panchkula, is a 100% state-owned agency of the government of Haryana in the Indian state of Haryana. For ease of doing business, Haryana is the first state in India to introduce a labour policy in 2005 and Land Pooling Policy in 2017, for which HSIIDC acts as the nodal agency.

image.alt: Haryana State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation

topPlaces.name: Chandimandir Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Chandimandir Cantonment is a military cantonment of the Indian Army located in Panchkula at the foot of the Sivalik Hills. It is the headquarters of the Western Command of the Indian Army.

topPlaces.name: Shivalik Enclave
topPlaces.description: Shivalik Enclave, also known as Sector 115, is situated between older sections of ManiMajra (also known as Manimajra Town) of Sector 13, Chandigarh, and Panchkula. It is located on the Kalka-Shimla Road next to the Housing Board Light Point.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Khooni Sahib
topPlaces.description: Sri Khooni Sahib is a gurudwara in Manimajra, a small town in Chandigarh, India. The gurdwara is located about one kilometer from the Mata Mansa Devi Temple, a Hindu temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nada Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nada Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nada Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nada Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nada Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nada Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Nada Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nada Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tau Devi Lal Athletic Stadium (Panchkula), Tau Devi Lal Cricket Stadium, Panchkula, National Cactus and Succulent Botanical Garden and Research Centre, Haryana State Industrial and Infrastructure Development Corporation.
